description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a vertical pole. The central housing is a rectangular black box containing three circular signal lights arranged vertically: red at the top, yellow in the middle, and green at the bottom. The lights are set within slightly protruding visors to shield them from sunlight and improve visibility. The pole is cylindrical and extends both above and below the signal housing, suggesting it is designed to be installed into the ground or onto a base. The surface appears to be made of painted or powder-coated metal, giving it a matte, durable finish suitable for outdoor environments. The overall design is utilitarian, focusing on visibility, durability, and weather resistance.
description: A black, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights (red, yellow, green) in a rectangular housing.
description_view_0: The traffic light is seen from the side, showing the slim profile of the pole and the rectangular housing.
description_view_1: A direct side view reveals the thinness of the pole and the protruding visors of the signal lights.
description_view_2: The front view displays all three signal lights (red, yellow, green) stacked vertically in the housing.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the protruding visors and the slim profile.
